explicit_text: I serve as a Campus Mentor for We Dignify, a pro-life nonprofit organization at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign. My role involves mentoring student leaders weekly, mostly through Zoom since I live in California, but I do visit the campus occasionally. We focus on developing leadership skills tailored to their specific roles, often discussing topics relevant to their group's activities. During the COVID-19 pandemic, we had to creatively adapt our leadership training and community building, including using pool noodles to maintain social distancing during in-person meetings.

My role involves mentoring student leaders weekly, mostly through Zoom since I live in California, but I do visit the campus occasionally. We focus on developing leadership skills tailored to their specific roles, often discussing topics relevant to their group's activities. During the health crisis starting in 2020, we had to creatively adapt our leadership training and community building, including using pool noodles to maintain social distancing during in-person meetings.

explicit_text: I work with student leaders at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign through the organization We Dignify. Although I live in California, I connect with students there weekly via Zoom and visit the campus once every month or two. The university is where our pro-life nonprofit operates, and we hold leadership trainings there, including during the summers. The campus setting is important for fostering community, which became challenging during the COVID-19 pandemic, but we found ways to maintain connection safely.
implicit_text: I work with student leaders at a major public university in Illinois through the organization I mentioned earlier. Although I live in California, I connect with students there weekly via Zoom and visit the campus once every month or two. This university is where our pro-life nonprofit operates, and we hold leadership trainings there, including during the summers. The campus setting is important for fostering community, which became challenging during the health crisis starting in 2020, but we found ways to maintain connection safely.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic really changed how we did mentoring and leadership training at We Dignify. Since we couldn't meet in person regularly, we shifted to Zoom meetings, which made building community more challenging. In the summer of 2021, we held an in-person leadership training with strict safety measures, including using pool noodles to ensure six feet of distance between participants. The pandemic forced us to be creative in maintaining connection and respect for different comfort levels while continuing our mission.
